# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Kong configuration for API routes that proxies to microservices. Configure upstream services, routes, and plugins for a production API gateway.
# Category: api_gateway_security

apiVersion: "konghq.org/v1"
kind: "KongPlugin"
metadata: 
 name: "proxy-to-services"
spec:  
 type: "nodejs"    # Required for plugin to work correctly in Kong version below v2.0.x, could be omitted if using the new plugins API (v1) or downgraded versions of Kong itself and above 3.5.6(e).
 data:           # Data field is required when a custom nodejs function needs defined here e..g {"proxy_to": "microservices"} - can also use other configuration values if necessary; not all fields are used depending on the plugin type, see Kong's documentation for more details 
   proxy-to: microservices     # The name of this field is a convention to define in custom plugins. Do NOT change it unless you know what your function will do with data from here! (mandatory) - e..g {"proxy_to": "microservices"} or other options can be used if necessary
   upstreams:            # Required for the plugin functionality, otherwise Kong won't recognize this field.  It should contain a list of all your services/routes to proxy requests from (mandatory) - e..g {"upstreams": ["service_one", "service_two"]}
     serviceOneURL: http://api-gateway or ip address for microservices one, port number if required.  # Replace with actual URL(http/https), not IP and Port numbers only (mandatory) - e..g {"upstreams": ["ip1", "port2"]}
     serviceTwoURL: http://api-gateway or ip address for microservices two, port number if required.  # Replace with actual URL(http/https), not IP and Port numbers only (mandatory) - e..g {"upstreams": ["ip1", "port2"]}
routes :            # Routes to define in Kong API Gateway for your microservices, it should contain a list of all routes that need proxying.  It will be defined as per the request path (mandatory) - e..g {"paths": ["/path1", "/path2"]}
   /api/users:        # Required to define in Kong API Gateway for your microservices, it should contain a list of all routes that need proxying.  It will be defined as per the request path (mandatory) - e..g { "paths": ["/path1", "/pat2"] }
     methods:            # Required to define in Kong API Gateway for your microservices, it should contain a list of all routes that need proxying.  It will be defined as per the request path (mandatory) - e..g {"methods": ["GET","POST", "DELETE"]}
       GET:               # Required to define in Kong API Gateway for your microservices, it should contain a list of all routes that need proxying.  It will be defined as per the request path (mandatory) - e..g {"methods": ["GET","POST", "DELETE"]}
         target:           # Required to define in Kong API Gateway for your microservices, it should contain a list of all routes that need proxying.  It will be defined as per the request path (mandatory) - e..g {"target": "/api/users"} or other options can also used if necessary
           service:         # Required to define in Kong API Gateway for your microservices, it should contain a list of all routes that need proxying.  It will be defined as per the request path (mandatory) - e..g {"service": "microservices_one"} or other options can also used if necessary
             name:           # Required to define in Kong API Gateway for your microservices, it should contain a list of all routes that need proxying.  It will be defined as per the request path (mandatory) - e..g {"name": "proxy_to"} or other options can also used if necessary
   /api/orders:        # Required to define in Kong API Gateway for your microservices, it should contain a list of all routes that need proxying.  It will be defined as per the request path (mandatory) - e..g { "paths": ["/path1", "/pat2"] }
     methods:            # Required to define in Kong API Gateway for your microservices, it should contain a list of all routes that need proxying.  It will be defined as per the request path (mandatory) - e..g {"methods": ["GET","POST", "DELETE"]}
       GET:               # Required to define in Kong API Gateway for your microservices, it should contain a list of all routes that need proxying.  It will be defined as per the request path (mandatory) - e..g {"methods": ["GET","POST", "DELETE"]}
         target:           # Required to define in Kong API Gateway for your microservices, it should contain a list of all routes that need proxying.  It will be defined as per the request path (mandatory) - e..g {"target": "/api/orders"} or other options can also used if necessary
           service:         # Required to define in Kong API Gateway for your microservices, it should contain a list of all routes that need proxying.  It will be defined as per the request path (mandatory) - e..g {"service": "microservices_two"} or other options can also used if necessary
             name:           # Required to define in Kong API Gateway for your microservices, it should contain a list of all routes that need proxying.  It will be defined as per the request path (mandatory) - e..g {"name": "proxy_to"} or other options can also used if necessary
